raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need tar archive of all subdirectories of /testbed, but without the prefix "testbed". Means we want to archive contents of /testbed/* but not include the top-level directory name? Use -C to change dir and then include subdirs. For each subdir, we want to archive them into /backup1.tar. So: tar -cf /backup1.tar -C /testbed .? But that would include all files and subdirs with relative paths. But we want only subdirectories, not the root? Maybe use find. Simpler: tar -cf /backup1.tar -C /testbed $(ls -d */) but that includes directories names. But prefix removal? If we use -C /testbed and then include subdirectories, the archive will have names like subdir/... which is fine. So command: tar -cf /backup1.tar -C /testbed $(find . -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -printf "%P\n")? Actually we want all subdirectories of /testbed, not nested. So: tar -cf /backup1.tar -C /testbed $(ls -d */) . But ls -d */ gives names with trailing slash. tar will include them. So final: tar -cf /backup1.tar -C /testbed $(ls -d */)
